row1.revenue: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a revenue of 716.9B USD, narrowly surpassing Walmart Inc. (WMT) by 3.7B USD. The top two companies demonstrate a significant revenue gap with the third-placed UnitedHealth Group Incorporated (UNH) at 313.7B USD.
row1.net_income: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with a net income of 132.2B USD, a significant 46% gap over NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) in second place. This substantial difference underscores GOOGL's dominant position in the tech industry.
row1.total_assets: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $4.4T in Total Assets, a significant gap of over $1T from the second-largest bank, Bank of America Corporation (BAC). This substantial difference underscores JPM's dominance in the financial sector.
row1.total_equity: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) leads the pack with $415.3B in Total Equity, a significant gap of $64.2B from Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) in second place.
row1.total_liabilities: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with Total Liabilities of 4.1T USD, a significant gap from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) at 3.1T USD, indicating substantial financial leverage. The top two companies' liabilities exceed the combined liabilities of the next three companies (AMZN, AAPL, MSFT) by over 1.5T USD.
row1.cash_and_equivalents: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM) leads the pack with $343.3B in Cash And Equivalents, a significant gap of over $100B from Bank of America Corporation (BAC) in second place. This substantial cash reserve underscores JPM's financial stability and potential for future investments or acquisitions.
row1.gross_profit: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) leads the pack with a Gross Profit of 360.5B USD, a significant 50% gap above the second-ranked Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) with 240.3B USD in Gross Profit.
row1.operating_income_ebit: AAPL (Apple Inc.) leads the pack with a significant Operating Income Ebit of $133.1B USD, outpacing NVDA (NVIDIA Corporation) by $3.7B USD. The top 4 companies account for over $480B USD in combined Operating Income Ebit, with a notable gap between leader AAPL and 4th place MSFT (Microsoft Corporation) at $4.6B USD.

row2_sector.revenue: Consumer Cyclical and Technology sectors dominate the revenue distribution with a combined 43.1% share, while Consumer Defensive and Communication Services sectors trail closely behind. The top two sectors account for nearly half of the total revenue.
row2_sector.total_assets: Financial Services sector dominates the sector distribution by Total Assets, accounting for 64.1% of the total, significantly outpacing the next closest sector. This concentration highlights the sector's substantial influence on the overall market.
row2_sector.net_income: Technology sector dominates the net income landscape, accounting for 39.2% of the total with $341.0B USD, significantly outpacing the next closest sector. Communication Services follows closely, with a 24.8% share, highlighting the concentrated nature of industry profitability.

row2_bubble: NVDA's 55.6% net margin significantly outpaces UNH's 2.7%, highlighting a stark contrast in profitability efficiency between the two companies. This disparity may be attributed to NVDA's strong pricing power and UNH's high operating expenses.
row3_heatmap: Morgan Stanley (MA) stands out for exceptional returns, boasting a 193.5% return on equity. Meanwhile, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) takes the opposite end, displaying significant leverage with a debt-to-equity ratio of 11.21x.
row4_market_trends: GOOGL's 101.7% return is a notable outlier, driven by its market dominance and innovation. In contrast, NFLX's -41.3% return reflects the challenges faced by the streaming industry. This dichotomy highlights the diverse performance of high-revenue companies in the past year.
